titleStmt.title: SII 22.61: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 61. (A.R. No. 61 of 1906) TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUMAYĀNAM. BRAHMAPURĪŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME MAṆḌAPA. KULŌTTUṄGA (III)—8th YEAR (A.D. 1185-6)

summary.p: The king is given the attribute “who was pleased to take Madurai”. This records a gift of 600 kuḻi or 6 mā of land at the dēvadāna village Tirumayānaviḷāgam after reclamation by Tillaināyaka-Perumāḷ of Kiḷiyūr-nāḍu in Pāṇḍikulāśanivaḷanāḍu for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple with the daily supply of an uḻakku of ghee.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tiribhuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ maturaikoṇṭaruḷiṉa śrīkulottuṅkaco
ḻatevarkku yāṇṭu 8 jeyaṅkoṇṭa coḻavaḷanāṭṭu Ākkūrnāṭṭu tirukkaṭavūr Uṭaiya
tirumaiyānamuṭaiya perumāḷkoyilil tirunantāviḷakku Eriya pāṇṭi kulācaṉivaḷanāṭṭu
kiḷiyūr na riraṅkuṭaiyāṉ tillaināyakaṉ perumāḷ Ittevar teva tāṉam tirumayānavi
ḷākattu maṇṇe ṟukollaiyil viḷainilam kalliṉa kuḻi 6 100 kuḻi Aṟuṉūṟuṉālnilam
Āṟumāvukkum naāḷ 1- kku ney n=ta Uḻakku Āka Eṭṭāvatu cittirai mātamutal Eriya Eṟiṉa tirununtā
viḷakku 1 Oṉṟu 2
Digital edition of SII 22.61 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
